story.asA: photographer using a mobile device or tablet
story.iWant: Recipe to work well on my screen size
story.soThat: I can convert presets on-the-go without needing my desktop computer

tasks.task: Create responsive.js module for touch detection and adaptations
Implement mobile-first CSS approach (base styles for mobile <768px)
Implement touch-specific styles (@media hover: none and pointer: coarse)
Implement adaptForTouch() for UI modifications (drop zone text, panel collapse)
Update drop zone text for mobile ("Tap to select your preset file")
Implement parameter panel auto-collapse on mobile (<768px)
Add touch-friendly tap targets (min 48×48px WCAG AAA)
Disable hover effects on touch devices
Update footer links to stack vertically on mobile
Test on real devices (iOS Safari, Android Chrome, iPad)

criterion.title: Mobile Breakpoint (<768px) - Single Column Layout
criterion.requirement: All content stacks vertically (single column)
Drop zone: full width (100% minus padding), smaller text, larger touch target (min 48×48px)
Parameter display: single column (parameter name/value stack), smaller fonts, collapsed by default
Format selector: full width options, larger radio buttons (easier to tap), format descriptions visible
Convert/Download buttons: full width, larger tap targets (min 48px height)
criterion.test: Resize browser to 375px width (iPhone size), verify all elements stack vertically, no horizontal scrolling, buttons easy to tap

criterion.title: Tablet Breakpoint (768-1023px) - Hybrid Layout
criterion.requirement: Drop zone: centered, 90% width (max 600px), full-size text
Parameter display: two-column grid (parameter name | value) OR single column if clearer
Format selector: centered, 90% width, larger radio buttons (touch-friendly)
Convert/Download buttons: centered, full width or fixed width (e.g., 400px)
criterion.test: Resize browser to 800px width (iPad size), verify layout adapts, comfortable touch targets, content readable without pinch-zoom

criterion.title: Desktop Breakpoint (≥1024px) - Full Layout (Already Implemented)
criterion.requirement: Drop zone: 600px width, centered
Parameter display: two-column grid
Format selector: 600px width, centered
All hover states functional
criterion.test: Resize browser to 1920px width (desktop), verify layout matches Stories 2-1 through 2-9 specifications, no changes needed

criterion.title: Touch-Friendly Interactions
criterion.requirement: No hover-dependent UX: essential actions work via tap (not just hover)
Larger tap targets: minimum 48×48px for all interactive elements (WCAG 2.1 AAA)
Spacing between tap targets: minimum 8px
No drag-drop on mobile: drag-drop disabled on touch devices, file picker opens on tap, show message "Tap to select file" (not "Drag or click")
criterion.test: Test on touch device (iPad, Android tablet), verify all buttons tappable, no accidental taps, tap drop zone → file picker opens

criterion.title: Mobile-Specific UI Adaptations
criterion.requirement: Drop zone text: Desktop ("Drop your preset file here or click to browse"), Mobile ("Tap to select your preset file")
Parameter panel: Desktop (expanded by default), Mobile (collapsed by default to save vertical space)
Error messages: Desktop (fixed position at top), Mobile (full-width at top, smaller dismiss button)
Footer: Desktop (horizontal links), Mobile (vertical links stacked)
criterion.test: Load on mobile device, verify drop zone text says "Tap to select", parameter panel collapsed, error message readable, footer links stacked

criterion.title: Performance on Mobile Devices
criterion.requirement: WASM loads <5s on 3G connection
Conversion completes <500ms on mobile (acceptable overhead)
Page renders <3s on 3G (First Contentful Paint)
No janky scrolling (60fps smooth scroll)
criterion.test: Test on real mobile device (or DevTools → throttle to "Slow 3G"), measure WASM load time, verify <5s, convert file → verify <500ms, scroll page → verify smooth

criterion.title: Mobile Testing (iOS Safari, Android Chrome)
criterion.requirement: iOS Safari: file picker works, WASM loads and runs, download works (saves to Files app)
Android Chrome: file picker works, WASM loads and runs, download works (saves to Downloads folder)
Mobile-specific bugs: no viewport zoom issues, no keyboard overlap (input fields visible when keyboard open), no broken layout on screen rotation
criterion.test: Test full conversion flow on iPhone (iOS Safari) and Android device (Chrome), verify file picker, conversion, download work, rotate device → verify layout adapts

criterion.title: Responsive Images and Icons
criterion.requirement: All icons scale properly (use SVG or CSS, not fixed-size PNG)
Format badges readable at all sizes
Privacy badge text readable on mobile (may need smaller font)
Error icons scale appropriately
criterion.test: Resize browser from 320px to 1920px, verify all icons/badges scale smoothly (no pixelation), text remains readable at all sizes

artifact.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
artifact.snippet: Medium story (1-2 days). Goal: Works on desktop and tablet. Breakpoints: Desktop ≥1024px (primary), Tablet 768-1023px (secondary), Mobile <768px (minimal support). Deliverables: Responsive CSS, touch-friendly controls (drag-drop, buttons), tested on actual devices. Success: Usable on iPad and similar tablets.

artifact.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
artifact.snippet: Safari iOS: Latest 2 versions, limited (mobile secondary). Chrome Android: Latest 2 versions, limited (mobile secondary). Desktop-first approach, mobile/tablet responsiveness as enhancement.

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: Mobile-responsive (works on tablets, though not primary use case). Responsive design (desktop-first, tablet-friendly). While Epic 2 is desktop-first, mobile/tablet responsiveness expands Recipe's usability for on-location use, tablet users, mobile browsing.

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: On-location: Photographer at shoot wants to quickly convert preset. Tablet users: iPad/Android tablet users (large screen, but touch input). Mobile browsing: User discovers Recipe on mobile, tries immediately.

artifact.title: Architecture
artifact.snippet: Desktop-first responsive design. Primary target: Desktop browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari). Secondary support: Tablets and mobile devices. Uses CSS media queries for responsive breakpoints.

artifact.title: Story 2-1: HTML Drag-Drop UI
artifact.snippet: Drop zone accepts file uploads via drag-drop or click. Desktop: "Drop your preset file here or click to browse". Will need mobile adaptation: "Tap to select your preset file".

artifact.title: Story 2-8: Error Handling UI
artifact.snippet: Error panel fixed position at top of page (z-index: 1000). Maximum width: 600px, 90% on mobile. Must adapt for mobile screens.

artifact.title: Story 2-9: Privacy Messaging
artifact.snippet: Privacy badge below main title. Must remain readable on mobile devices (may need smaller font or adjusted layout).

artifact.reason: Primary file for responsive design. Will add mobile-first base styles, tablet breakpoint (@media min-width: 768px), desktop breakpoint (@media min-width: 1024px), touch-specific styles (@media hover: none).
Central initialization point. Will integrate responsive.js module initialization.

artifact.symbol: viewport meta tag, all responsive containers
artifact.reason: Must include viewport meta tag for mobile rendering. All HTML elements need responsive-friendly structure (semantic markup, flexible containers).
Story 2-2 handles drag-drop. May need to disable drag events on touch devices, rely on click/tap for file picker.
Story 2-4 displays parameters. May need to add collapse/expand functionality for mobile (save vertical space).
Story 2-8 handles errors. Error panel must adapt to mobile screens (full-width, smaller dismiss button).
Story 2-9 privacy messaging. Privacy badge and FAQ must be readable/functional on mobile.

javascript.package: classList, querySelector for responsive UI updates

browser.requirement: Required for touch device detection and handling

story-dependencies.story: Drop zone must adapt to mobile (tap instead of drag)
File picker must work on mobile/tablet devices
Parameter panel must collapse on mobile (save vertical space)
Format selector must be touch-friendly (larger tap targets)
WASM must load and perform on mobile devices (3G, low-end hardware)
Download button must be touch-friendly, work on mobile browsers
Error panel must adapt to mobile screens (full-width, smaller dismiss)
Privacy badge and FAQ must be readable/functional on mobile

constraints.constraint: Desktop-first design philosophy - mobile is enhancement, not primary target
Mobile-first CSS implementation - base styles for mobile, enhance for desktop
Minimum tap target size: 48×48px (WCAG 2.1 AAA standard)
No hover-dependent functionality - all essential actions work via tap
Must include viewport meta tag: width=device-width, initial-scale=1
Must work on iOS Safari (latest 2 versions)
Must work on Android Chrome (latest 2 versions)
Must handle screen rotation without breaking layout
No horizontal scrolling at any breakpoint
Text readable without pinch-zoom on all devices

interface.name: isTouchDevice()
interface.description: Detects if device supports touch input. Checks ontouchstart, maxTouchPoints, msMaxTouchPoints. Returns true for touch-enabled devices.

interface.name: adaptForTouch()
interface.description: Adapts UI for touch devices: updates drop zone text to "Tap to select", collapses parameter panel on mobile, disables unreliable drag-drop events. Called on page load if touch detected.

interface.name: handleOrientationChange()
interface.description: Handles device orientation changes (portrait ↔ landscape). Listens for orientationchange event, triggers UI re-render if needed (most handled by CSS).

interface.name: initializeResponsive()
interface.description: Initializes all responsive adaptations. Calls adaptForTouch() and handleOrientationChange(). Called from main.js on DOMContentLoaded.

interface.name: CSS Media Queries
interface.description: CSS breakpoints for responsive design. Base styles for mobile (<768px), tablet styles (768-1023px), desktop styles (≥1024px), touch-specific styles (@media hover: none).

interface.name: viewport meta tag
interface.description: Controls viewport rendering on mobile devices. Required for responsive design - ensures 1:1 pixel ratio, prevents unwanted zooming.

interface.name: window.matchMedia()
interface.description: JavaScript media query matching. Can detect breakpoint changes in real-time. Optional for dynamic responsive behavior.

standards.standard: Real device testing (iPhone iOS Safari, Android Chrome, iPad Safari)
Touch interaction testing (tap targets, spacing, no accidental taps)
Screen rotation testing (portrait ↔ landscape, verify no broken layouts)
Visual verification at all breakpoints (no horizontal scroll, readable text)
Accessibility testing (WCAG 2.1 AAA tap target compliance, keyboard navigation)
Cross-browser testing (Chrome, Firefox, Safari on desktop, iOS Safari, Android Chrome on mobile)

locations.location: web/static/responsive.js (unit tests can be added inline or separate test file)
Browser DevTools Responsive Design Mode (test all breakpoints)
Real mobile devices (iPhone, Android phone, iPad for actual touch testing)
DevTools Network tab (throttle to 3G, measure WASM load time)
DevTools Performance tab (profile scroll performance, verify 60fps)

ideas.idea: Test mobile layout (375px): Resize browser to iPhone size, verify single column, no horizontal scroll, full-width buttons
Test extreme mobile (320px): iPhone SE size, verify UI still usable, no broken layout
Test tablet layout (800px): Resize to iPad size, verify comfortable layout, touch-friendly, readable without zoom
Test desktop layout (1920px): Verify matches Stories 2-1 through 2-9, no unintended changes
Test large desktop (2560px): Verify content doesn't stretch awkwardly, max-width enforced
Test touch targets: Use ruler/measuring tool to verify all interactive elements ≥48×48px
Test tap spacing: Verify ≥8px spacing between buttons, no accidental taps on real device
Test drag-drop on touch: Tap drop zone on iPad, verify file picker opens (drag-drop doesn't interfere)
Test drop zone text: Load on mobile, verify says "Tap to select" (not "Drop or click")
Test parameter panel: Load on mobile, verify collapsed by default (expand/collapse works)
Test footer links: Verify stack vertically on mobile, horizontal on desktop
Test WASM load (3G): DevTools → throttle to Slow 3G, measure load time, verify <5s
Test conversion performance: Convert file on mobile, measure time, verify <500ms
Test scroll performance: DevTools Performance tab, scroll page, verify 60fps (no jank)
Test iOS Safari: Complete full conversion flow on iPhone, verify file picker, WASM, download work
Test Android Chrome: Complete full conversion flow on Android, verify all functionality
Test screen rotation: Rotate device portrait ↔ landscape, verify layout adapts, no broken elements
Test keyboard overlap: Focus input field (if any) on mobile, verify visible when keyboard opens
Test icon scaling: Resize browser 320px → 1920px, verify all icons/badges scale smoothly (SVG)
Test privacy badge: Verify readable at all sizes, may need smaller font on mobile
Test all breakpoints systematically: 320, 375, 414, 768, 1024, 1280, 1920, 2560px
Test complete user flow on mobile: Upload → detect format → select target → convert → download (end-to-end)
Test error messages on mobile: Trigger error, verify readable, dismiss button tappable
Test privacy FAQ on mobile: Expand/collapse FAQ, verify readable, links tappable
Test format selector on mobile: Tap radio buttons, verify easy to select (large tap targets)
Test parameter display on mobile: Expand/collapse panel, verify single-column layout clear
